Title: Register a new user with valid information
Preconditions: User is not logged in. Test email does not already exist.
Steps:
1. Open the mobile application.
2. Tap Create Account or Register.
3. Enter valid name, email, and password.
4. Submit the registration form.
Expected Result:
1. User account is created successfully.
2. User is redirected to the login screen or home dashboard.
3. Backend stores the user with a hashed password, not plain text.

Title: Reject registration with an already used email
Preconditions: A user account already exists with the test email.
Steps:
1. Open the registration screen.
2. Enter the existing email with valid password and name.
3. Submit the form.
Expected Result:
1. Application displays a clear duplicate-email error.
2. No second account is created.
3. User remains on the registration screen.

Title: Reject registration when required fields are empty
Preconditions: User is on the registration screen.
Steps:
1. Leave name, email, or password empty.
2. Submit the form.
Expected Result:
1. Application displays required-field validation messages.
2. Request is not accepted by the backend.
3. User account is not created.

Title: Login with valid credentials
Preconditions: A registered user exists.
Steps:
1. Open the login screen.
2. Enter valid email and password.
3. Tap Login.
Expected Result:
1. User is authenticated successfully.
2. Application navigates to the dashboard.
3. A valid session token is stored securely on the device.

Title: Reject login with invalid password
Preconditions: A registered user exists.
Steps:
1. Open the login screen.
2. Enter the registered email and an incorrect password.
3. Tap Login.
Expected Result:
1. Application displays an invalid credentials message.
2. User is not logged in.
3. No protected product data is shown.

Title: Logout clears user session
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Steps:
1. Open Settings or Profile.
2. Tap Logout.
3. Confirm logout if prompted.
4. Close and reopen the application.
Expected Result:
1. User is logged out.
2. Session token is cleared or invalidated.
3. Application opens to the login screen and does not show private product data.

Title: Block access to protected API without token
Preconditions: Backend API is running.
Steps: 1. Send a request to a protected product endpoint without an authentication token.
Expected Result:
1. Backend returns 401 Unauthorized or equivalent.
2. No product data is returned.

Title: Display dashboard summary after login
Preconditions: User is logged in and has at least one saved product.
Steps:
1. Log in to the mobile app.
2. Open the dashboard screen.
Expected Result:
1. Dashboard loads successfully.
2. Application displays product summary information such as total products, expired items, and soon-to-expire items.
3. Only the current user’s product data is shown.

Title: Show empty state when user has no products
Preconditions: User is logged in and has no saved products.
Steps: 1. Open the dashboard or product list screen.
Expected Result:
1. Application displays a helpful empty-state message.
2. Application provides a clear option to add a product manually.

Title: Add product manually with all valid fields
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Steps:
1. Open Add Product screen.
2. Enter product name, category, quantity, storage location, purchase date, and expiration date manually.
3. Tap Save.
Expected Result:
1. Product is saved successfully.
2. Product appears in the product list.
3. Product data is stored in the backend database under the current user.

Title: Add product manually with only required fields
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Steps:
1. Open Add Product screen.
2. Enter only required fields such as product name and expiration date.
3. Tap Save.
Expected Result:
1. Product is saved successfully if required fields are valid.
2. Optional fields remain blank or use default values.
3. Product appears in the product list.

Title: Reject product save when name is missing
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Steps:
1. Open Add Product screen.
2. Leave product name empty.
3. Enter a valid expiration date.
4. Tap Save.
Expected Result:
1. Application displays a product-name validation message.
2. Product is not saved.

Title: Reject product save when expiration date is missing
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Steps:
1. Open Add Product screen.
2. Enter product name.
3. Leave expiration date empty.
4. Tap Save.
Expected Result:
1. Application displays an expiration-date validation message.
2. Product is not saved.

Title: Allow past expiration date and mark product expired
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Steps:
1. Open Add Product screen.
2. Enter valid product information.
3. Set expiration date to a date before today.
4. Tap Save.
Expected Result:
1. Product is saved successfully or user is warned before saving based on design.
2. Product status appears as Expired in the list/dashboard.

Title: Prevent invalid quantity values
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Steps:
1. Open Add Product screen.
2. Enter a negative quantity or invalid text in the quantity field.
3. Tap Save.
Expected Result:
1. Application displays a quantity validation message.
2. Invalid quantity is not saved.

Title: Edit existing product details
Preconditions: User is logged in and has at least one product.
Steps:
1. Open the product list.
2. Select an existing product.
3. Tap Edit.
4. Change product name, quantity, category, location, or expiration date.
5. Tap Save.
Expected Result:
1. Updated product details are saved successfully.
2. Product list and product detail screen show the new information.
3. Backend database reflects the update.

Title: Cancel edit without saving changes
Preconditions: User is logged in and has at least one product.
Steps:
1. Open an existing product.
2. Tap Edit.
3. Change one or more fields.
4. Tap Cancel or go back without saving.
Expected Result:
1. Application returns to the previous screen.
2. Original product data remains unchanged.

Title: Delete an existing product after confirmation
Preconditions: User is logged in and has at least one product.
Steps:
1. Open the product detail screen.
2. Tap Delete.
3. Confirm the delete action.
Expected Result:
1. Product is removed from the list.
2. Product is deleted or marked inactive in the backend.
3. Dashboard summary updates accordingly.

Title: Cancel product delete confirmation
Preconditions: User is logged in and has at least one product.
Steps:
1. Open the product detail screen.
2. Tap Delete.
3. Cancel the confirmation prompt.
Expected Result:
1. Product is not deleted.
2. User remains on the product detail screen or returns safely.

Title: View all saved products
Preconditions: User is logged in and has multiple products.
Steps: 1. Open the Products screen.
Expected Result:
1. All products belonging to the current user are displayed.
2. Each product shows key information such as name, expiration date, and status.

Title: Sort products by expiration date
Preconditions: User is logged in and has multiple products with different expiration dates.
Steps:
1. Open the Products screen.
2. Select sorting by expiration date.
Expected Result:
1. Products are ordered by expiration date.
2. Soonest expiration dates appear first if ascending sort is selected.

Title: Filter products by expiration status
Preconditions: User is logged in and has active, soon-to-expire, and expired products.
Steps:
1. Open the Products screen.
2. Apply the Expired filter.
3. Apply the Soon to Expire filter.
Expected Result:
1. Expired filter shows only expired products.
2. Soon to Expire filter shows only products within the configured alert range.
3. Removing filters restores the full list.

Title: Search products by name
Preconditions: User is logged in and has multiple products.
Steps:
1. Open the Products screen.
2. Enter a product name or partial name in the search field.
Expected Result:
1. Product list updates to show matching products.
2. Non-matching products are hidden.
3. Empty search restores the full list.

Title: Open product detail screen
Preconditions: User is logged in and has at least one product.
Steps:
1. Open the product list.
2. Tap a product item.
Expected Result:
1. Product detail screen opens.
2. Screen shows full saved product information.
3. Edit and delete actions are available if allowed.

Title: Mark product as expired when expiration date has passed
Preconditions: User has a product with an expiration date before today.
Steps: 1. Open dashboard or product list.
Expected Result:
1. Product status is displayed as Expired.
2. Expired product is included in expired count or expired filter.

Title: Mark product as soon to expire based on alert threshold
Preconditions: User has a product expiring within the configured alert threshold.
Steps: 1. Open dashboard or product list.
Expected Result:
1. Product status is displayed as Soon to Expire or equivalent.
2. Product is included in soon-to-expire count/filter.

Title: Display product as active when expiration is outside threshold
Preconditions: User has a product with expiration date after the alert threshold.
Steps: 1. Open dashboard or product list.
Expected Result:
1. Product status is displayed as Active or Safe.
2. Product is not shown in expired or soon-to-expire filters.

Title: Create expiration notification for soon-to-expire product
Preconditions: User is logged in, notifications are enabled, and a product is near expiration.
Steps:
1. Run scheduled expiration check or trigger backend notification job.
2. Open the app notification area or observe device notification.
Expected Result:
1. Notification is generated for the correct product.
2. Notification includes useful information such as product name and expiration date.
3. Notification belongs only to the owning user.

Title: Do not create duplicate notification for same product and date
Preconditions: A notification already exists for the product for the current alert period.
Steps: 1. Run the scheduled expiration check again for the same product.
Expected Result:
1. Backend does not create duplicate notifications for the same product alert.
2. User receives only one relevant alert.

Title: Disable notifications from settings
Preconditions: User is logged in and notifications are enabled.
Steps:
1. Open Settings.
2. Turn off expiration notifications.
3. Save settings.
4. Run expiration check for a soon-to-expire product.
Expected Result:
1. User preference is saved.
2. No push or in-app notification is generated for that user while disabled.

Title: Update expiration alert threshold
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Steps:
1. Open Settings.
2. Change alert threshold, for example from 7 days to 3 days.
3. Save settings.
Expected Result:
1. New threshold is saved.
2. Soon-to-expire status and notifications use the updated threshold.

Title: Persist user settings after app restart
Preconditions: User is logged in and has changed settings.
Steps:
1. Change alert threshold or notification preference.
2. Close the application.
3. Reopen and log in if needed.
4. Open Settings.
Expected Result:
1. Previously saved settings are displayed correctly.
2. Application behavior follows the saved settings.

Title: Create product through API with valid authenticated request
Preconditions: Backend is running and valid auth token is available.
Steps: 1. Send POST request to product create endpoint with valid product JSON and auth token.
Expected Result:
1. API returns success status such as 201 Created.
2. Response includes created product data or product ID.
3. Database contains the new product linked to the authenticated user.

Title: Reject product API request with missing required fields
Preconditions: Backend is running and valid auth token is available.
Steps: 1. Send POST request to product create endpoint without product name or expiration date.
Expected Result:
1. API returns validation error status such as 400 Bad Request.
2. Response explains the missing or invalid field.
3. Database is not changed.

Title: Return only current user products from product list API
Preconditions: Two users exist, each with different products.
Steps:
1. Authenticate as User A.
2. Call product list endpoint.
Expected Result:
1. API returns only User A products.
2. User B products are not included in the response.

Title: Prevent user from editing another user product
Preconditions: Two users exist. User B owns a product.
Steps:
1. Authenticate as User A.
2. Send update request for User B product ID.
Expected Result:
1. API returns 403 Forbidden or 404 Not Found.
2. User B product remains unchanged.

Title: Prevent user from deleting another user product
Preconditions: Two users exist. User B owns a product.
Steps:
1. Authenticate as User A.
2. Send delete request for User B product ID.
Expected Result:
1. API returns 403 Forbidden or 404 Not Found.
2. User B product remains available.

Title: Store product data with correct schema fields
Preconditions: Backend and database are running.
Steps:
1. Create a product using the app or API.
2. Inspect the product record in the database.
Expected Result:
1. Record contains expected fields such as id, user_id, name, category, quantity, location, purchase_date, expiration_date, created_at, and updated_at.
2. Record does not contain barcode or AI scan fields for the current project scope.

Title: Delete or archive product consistently
Preconditions: User has at least one product in the database.
Steps:
1. Delete the product from the app.
2. Inspect the database record or product list endpoint.
Expected Result:
1. Deleted product no longer appears in user-facing product list.
2. Database state matches the chosen design: hard deleted or marked inactive.

Title: Display validation errors clearly on mobile screens
Preconditions: User is on Add Product or Login screen.
Steps: 1. Submit a form with invalid or missing fields.
Expected Result:
1. Validation messages appear near the related fields or in a clear alert.
2. Messages are readable on mobile screen sizes.
3. User can correct the form without losing already entered valid data.

Title: Handle network error when backend is unavailable
Preconditions: Mobile app is installed and backend service is stopped or unreachable.
Steps: 1. Open the app and attempt login or product list loading.
Expected Result:
1. Application displays a friendly network error message.
2. Application does not crash.
3. User can retry after service is restored.

Title: Show loading state while product data is loading
Preconditions: User is logged in and network is slow.
Steps: 1. Open dashboard or product list during slow API response.
Expected Result:
1. Application shows loading indicator or skeleton state.
2. Application updates with product data after response is received.

Title: Start project services with Docker Compose
Preconditions: docker-compose.yml and Dockerfiles are configured.
Steps:
1. Run docker compose up --build.
2. Check running services.
Expected Result:
1. Database container starts successfully.
2. Backend container starts successfully and connects to database.
3. Mobile/Expo development service starts if included in the compose profile.

Title: Backend reads environment variables from container configuration
Preconditions: Docker Compose environment variables are configured.
Steps:
1. Start backend container.
2. Check backend logs and health endpoint if available.
Expected Result:
1. Backend uses configured database host, port, username, password, and JWT secret.
2. No hard-coded local-only configuration is required for container startup.

Title: Manual-entry-only scope does not show barcode or AI scanning options
Preconditions: Application is installed with current project scope.
Steps:
1. Open dashboard, add product screen, product detail screen, and settings.
2. Review visible actions and fields.
Expected Result:
1. No barcode scanning button is displayed.
2. No AI scanning/image recognition option is displayed.
3. Product creation is completed by manual entry only.
